overview.minides: Maserati's first-ever SUV — available with three powertrains including a 48V mild-hybrid and a Ferrari-sourced V8, all paired with AWD and active air suspension.
overview.description: The Maserati Levante is the Italian marque's first SUV, originally launched in India in 2018 and updated with a new powertrain lineup thereafter. It is currently offered in three configurations: the GT Hybrid with a 2.0-litre turbocharged petrol engine mated to a 48V mild-hybrid system (330 PS / 450 Nm), the Modena with a Nettuno-derived 3.0-litre twin-turbo V6 producing 430 PS and 580 Nm, and the range-topping Trofeo powered by a Ferrari-sourced 3.8-litre twin-turbo V8 generating 580 PS / 730 Nm, capable of reaching 326 km/h. All variants use an 8-speed automatic, full-time AWD, and active air suspension. The cabin features frameless doors (a sports car carry-over), a panoramic sunroof, full leather upholstery, 12-way power front seats, and an infotainment system with optional Harman Kardon or Bowers & Wilkins audio. The Trofeo adds a Corsa drive mode with launch control, carbon-ceramic Brembo brakes, carbon-fibre exterior accents, and Pieno Fiore natural full-grain leather.

suspension.front: Double Wishbone with Active Air Suspension
suspension.additionalTech: Active Suspension with Skyhook Damping, Height Adjustable (+45 mm)
